key_insight: D-gun applied Al2O3-TiO2 coating significantly improves the wear resistance of Ti-6Al-4V, and random forest gives the most accurate wear prediction among the tested machine learning models.
engineering_relevance: This supports using detonation-sprayed ceramic coatings to extend the service life of titanium components in aerospace, biomedical, and marine tribological applications.
method: Applied ~200 µm Al2O3-TiO2 coating on Ti-6Al-4V using D-gun processing.
Evaluated wear with ASTM G99 pin-on-disc tests against EN31 steel under 40–100 N, 100–200 rpm, and 20–40 min.
Built ANN, KNN, and random forest models to predict wear from sliding parameters.
evidence_signal: Experimental wear tests and SEM observations showed reduced wear and protective tribo-layer formation on coated surfaces, while random forest achieved the lowest mean percentage error and mean squared error.
watch_item: The ML models are based on a limited set of test conditions, so prediction reliability may drop outside the studied load, speed, and time ranges.
paper_title: Tribological enhancement of Al 2 O 3 -TiO 2 D-gun coated Ti-6Al-4V alloy: A machine learning approach
